exemplar.title: Exploring the 2D Area of the FIFA World Cup Trophy

detailedExemplarData.title: Exploring the 2D Area of the FIFA World Cup Trophy

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear academic flow with recognizable structure (introduction, methodology, exploration, conclusion).
Use of headings, table of contents and page numbers provides easy navigation.
Coherent logical development where arguments proceed in a cause-and-effect sequence.
weaknesses: Explanations are sometimes verbose and repetitive, affecting conciseness.
Sections of historical background and repeated RÂ² definitions are not directly relevant.
Inconsistent page numbering in contents (e.g., bibliography).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration demonstrates solid coherence and logical progression, with an orderly structure that guides the reader effectively. However, verbosity in explanations and some irrelevant background material prevent the work from reaching the highest standard of conciseness. Overall, a Band 3 performance in Presentation is justified.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (tables, diagrams, coloured overlays) to support understanding.
Detailed integration steps are clearly laid out in a logical sequence.
Precise rounding of regression outcomes to four decimal places enhances clarity.
weaknesses: Mathematical notation is inconsistent: units missing or superscripts misplaced.
Some long derivations contain transcription mistakes and missing final numeric evaluations.
Inline notation for function domains is dense and could be better formatted.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is evidence of correct use of mathematical language and a variety of representations, but inconsistencies in notation, missing units and errors in derivations limit clarity. The communication is partially effective, meriting a Band 2 score.

strengths: Strong personal connection to the topic through the studentâs interest in football.
Evidence of independent thinking in designing a unique point-by-point modelling approach.
Use of RÂ² diagnostics and manual area corrections shows initiative beyond minimal requirements.
weaknesses: Creativity is somewhat repetitive, relying on similar regression techniques without a fundamentally novel strategy.
Personal approach is clear but does not push boundaries with innovative methodology.
Opportunities for further personal insight (e.g. alternate modelling techniques) are not fully explored.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration reflects clear personal engagement and independent thinking, with a defined personal approach. However, the level of creativity and initiative remains moderate rather than exceptional, justifying a Band 2 score.

strengths: Includes some reflection on future 3D modelling, showing awareness of methodological limitations.
weaknesses: Reflection is intermittent and largely descriptive rather than critically analytical.
Discussion of negative RÂ² and error propagation is absent, limiting depth of analysis.
Evaluation of outcomes is brief, without quantified error analysis or concrete improvement plans.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While there are moments of reflection, the analysis of processes and outcomes lacks depth and critical insight. The limited and basic nature of the reflection warrants a Band 1 score.

strengths: Relevant SL mathematics used (integration, quartic regression, scale factors) directly addresses the research question.
Procedural steps such as sum of integrals and area decompositions are carried out correctly in many parts.
Awareness of regression diagnostics (RÂ² calculations) demonstrates appropriate use of mathematical tools.
weaknesses: Conceptual misunderstandings: negative RÂ² and unit inconsistencies weaken accuracy.
Arithmetic errors in area subtraction and boundary interval continuity issues are present.
GeoGebra methodology lacks sufficient detail on coordinate scaling and axis setup.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student applies appropriate SL-level mathematics in context, but conceptual errors and procedural inaccuracies limit the depth of understanding. This performance aligns with a middle-band (Band 2) score for Use of Mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: While the overall communication line is visible, several long derivations include transcription mistakes (negative RÂ² explanations) and the appendix overwhelms the main argument.  Logical flow is thus partially clear but not consistently sharp.

The student demonstrates partial understandingâset-up of integrals is correct, but negative RÂ², unit inconsistencies and area-subtraction arithmetic errors indicate some conceptual and procedural weaknesses, so accuracy and depth are only fair.

The student employs tables, point lists, diagrams, coloured overlays and algebraic expressions to depict the same ideas; these multiple representations work together effectively to support understanding, even if aesthetic quality varies.

The investigation follows a recognizable academic flow (introduction-methodology-exploration-conclusion) and the arguments generally proceed in a cause-and-effect sequence (data collection â modelling â integration â scaling).  Although transitions are sometimes verbose or repetitive, the reader can follow the mathematical story without major confusion, indicating solidâthough not exemplaryâlogical development.

Using quartic regressions, RÂ² diagnostics and manual area corrections demonstrates initiative beyond a minimal approach, yet creativity is restrained by very similar repeated techniques and no fundamentally novel strategy.
The student comments on high vs low RÂ² and states whether the hypothesis was confirmed, yet stops short of probing why negative RÂ² emerged or how errors propagate; analytical commentary is basic.

Evaluation is limited to brief statements about potential 3-D modelling and comparing with other trophies; there is no quantified error analysis or concrete plan for improvement, so evaluation remains limited.
Some reflection appearsâe.g. acknowledgement of scaling limitations and suggestions for 3-D modellingâbut depth is intermittent and largely descriptive rather than fully critical.

Mathematical symbols (â«, RÂ², quartic form) are used, but inconsistentlyâunits are sometimes missing and superscripts occasionally misplaced.  Notation such as ây = f1(x) â gx(x)= â¦â is confusing.  Hence language is acceptable yet not mostly consistent.

The exploration is clearly framed around the studentâs passion for football, and the hands-on photograph-to-Geogebra approach indicates a personal route rather than a textbook procedure, giving clearâif not strikingâpersonal ownership.

Explanations are lengthy and sometimes drift (e.g. historical background of viewership, repeated definition of RÂ²); nevertheless most material still relates to modelling or evaluation.  The text could clearly be tightened and some duplicated screenshots removed, so conciseness is moderate rather than strong.

Integration, polynomial regression, scale factors and area ratios are all relevant to SL and applied in context; despite some extraneous history, the mathematics selected directly addresses the question.

Choosing a personally owned replica, designing unique point-by-point modelling and contradicting an initial hypothesis show noticeable independent thought, though these moves remain within common IA practice.

Headings, a contents table and page numbers provide clear navigation; each major task (data capture, fitting, area subtraction) sits in its own labelled section.  Minor lapses such as the bibliography appearing on âPage 2â of the contents and lengthy appendix placement keep the organisation from being outstanding, but overall structure is orderly and easy to trace.
Quartic regression, definite integrals and similarity scaling are clearly within SL scope and deployed appropriately; the level therefore matches course expectations.

annotations.comment: The title and research question are articulated clearly, establishing a focused investigation into material use efficiency. This strong framing guides the reader on the studyâs intent from the outset.
The research question is clear but could specify the quantitative metric (e.g. 2D area ratio) to sharpen the focus on material use efficiency.
The table of contents shows a mismatch in page numbering for the bibliography entry (listed as Page 2). Ensure consistency to aid navigation.
The studentâs personal connection and passion for football are convincingly linked to the choice of topic, demonstrating genuine engagement.
The introductory background on viewership statistics, while interesting, is not directly relevant to the mathematical modeling and could be condensed.
The methodology section effectively situates your approach in the context of existing research, demonstrating a strong literature linkage.
The description of GeoGebra usage lacks specific details on coordinate scaling and axis setup; include these to enhance reproducibility.
The inline notation for function domains is dense and could be reformatted into a clear table or separate lines for improved mathematical communication.
The assumption of quartic and linear forms for the contour functions is clearly stated, reflecting thoughtful classification of model segments.
Pointâdensity concerns are noted, but a brief discussion on the trade-off between point number and regression accuracy would strengthen the modeling rationale.
The standard quartic and linear function forms are given, but a justification for choosing quartic over lower-degree polynomials is missing.
Figure 2 provides an effective visual preview of expected functions, aiding clarity through a multiple-representation approach.
The list of regression outcomes is meticulously rounded to 4 dp, promoting precision and clarity in the presentation of functions.
There is no commentary on the negative leading coefficients or their impact on curve shape; interpret these signs to deepen mathematical understanding.
Figures 5 and 6 showcase model applications of quartic and linear functions effectively, supporting the logical structure of the exploration.
Image axes and scales are not always annotated; include axis labels for clarity in your GeoGebra model screenshots.
The choice of intervals for each function is documented clearly, but overlapping or small gaps could affect the enclosed area; verify boundary continuity.
Including the RÂ² calculations demonstrates awareness of regression diagnostics and reflects appropriate use of mathematics to gauge model accuracy.
Negative RÂ² results are calculated but not interpreted; discuss why RSS>TSS produces negative values and its implication for curve fit.
The detailed integration steps are presented clearly, enhancing mathematical communication through a logical sequence of expressions.
The final numeric evaluation of the integral is omitted; include the computed value immediately after the antiderivative to complete the derivation.
Sum of integrals is computed correctly, leading to a total area including extra parts, which shows strong procedural accuracy.
The rationale for dividing extra parts into areas A, B, and C needs elaboration; specify why these regions capture all non-model segments.
There is an arithmetic inconsistency in Area A: the triangle1 area is listed as 9 cmÂ² without explanation, and the total sum formula is incorrect.
Area C decomposition with triangles and rectangle is consistently applied, reinforcing systematic problem-solving approach.
Model height is reported in cmÂ² rather than cm; correct units to maintain mathematical communication integrity.
The conclusionâs interpretation of the radius efficiency ratio could be clarified; a value <1 indicates more efficient material use, not less.
Reflection on future 3D modeling shows thoughtful evaluation, indicating an awareness of methodological limitations and possible improvements.
Bibliography entries vary in formatting style and lack consistent indentation; apply a standard citation format for professionalism.
